prompt: create a counter using 7 segment display. every time user hit button, the number increse, after 9 goes back to 0

trace.text:
Your 7-segment button counter is ready! 🎉 Here's a full breakdown:

---

## 📌 Wiring Table

| Arduino Pin | Component | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| **D2** | R_A → Segment A | Top bar |
| **D3** | R_B → Segment B | Top-right |
| **D4** | R_C → Segment C | Bottom-right |
| **D5** | R_D → Segment D | Bottom bar |
| **D6** | R_E → Segment E | Bottom-left |
| **D7** | R_F → Segment F | Top-left |
| **D8** | R_G → Segment G | Middle bar |
| **GND** | Display common | Common cathode |
| **D9** | Button (one side) | Increment input |
| **GND** | Button (other side) | With internal pull-up |

Each segment has a **220Ω series resistor** for current limiting.

---

## 🔢 How It Works

- **Button press** is detected via `INPUT_PULLUP` — no external pull-up resistor needed.
- A **30ms debounce delay** prevents false triggers from contact bounce.
- Counter increments **0 → 1 → 2 → … → 9 → 0** (wraps around).
- Digits are displayed using an `if/else` lookup table for each of the 7 segments.

---

## ⚡ Power Budget Summary

| Item | Current |
|---|---|
| Each lit segment (5V − 2V) / 220Ω | ~13.6 mA |
| Max segments ON at once (digit 8, all 7 lit) | ~95 mA |
| Typical digit (avg ~4–5 segments) | ~55–68 mA |
| Button pin | < 1 mA |
| **Total draw** | **≤ ~96 mA** |

> ✅ Each individual pin stays **well under 20mA** — each segment draws ~13.6mA. Total board draw is within the Arduino Uno's safe 500mA USB limit.

issues: BTN (button) has Arduino signal wires on both sides. Use one side for input and the opposite side for power/ground reference.
